nativeRange.habitat: Wet, shaded margins, seeps, and stream banks in the southeastern and eastern United States; reported to tolerate seasonal emersion and is regularly grown emersed in greenhouse culture

decorPreferences: open midground or trimmed foreground space
moderate to strong lighting for compact, bushy form
stable water column with regular liquid fertilisation

avoidWith: boisterous diggers that uproot stem plants

summary: Micranthemum umbrosum ('Giant Baby Tears') is a bright-green small-leaved stem plant native to the eastern United States, with leaves visibly larger and rounder than Pearl Weed or HC dwarf baby tears. Generally rated intermediate; tends to perform best with moderate-to-strong light, nutrient-rich substrate, and supplemental CO2.
careNotes: Micranthemum umbrosum — sold in the hobby as 'Giant Baby Tears', 'Baby Tears Giant', or occasionally 'Shade Mudflower' — is a creeping/branching stem plant in the family Linderniaceae (order Lamiales) native to wet, shaded margins of the southeastern and eastern United States. It is one of three Micranthemum species commonly found in aquascaping and is the largest-leaved of the trio: leaves are noticeably larger and rounder than those of M. micranthemoides ('Pearl Weed', fw-plant-041) and dramatically larger than Hemianthus / Micranthemum callitrichoides ('HC' / Dwarf Baby Tears, fw-plant-027). Buce Plant explicitly markets it as the Pearl Weed alternative with 'slightly larger and rounder' leaves. In the trade Buce and Modern Aquarium rate it 'easy' to 'easy-to-moderate', while Tropica and Aquasabi rate it 'medium' — averaging to intermediate. Typical submerged growth is 5–15 cm tall when trimmed, with emersed/untrimmed specimens documented to 20–30 cm; we record 5–20 cm. Temperature tolerance is broad — Modern Aquarium cites 72–86 °F (~22–30 °C) and Aquasabi gives an extremely wide 4–26 °C that likely includes emergent conditions; we use ~20–28 °C as the practical aquarium range. pH is reported as adaptable (Buce) or 5–7 (Aquasabi), and the plant tolerates very soft to moderately hard water (gH ~0–30 °dGH per Aquasabi). Lighting is best at medium-to-strong levels: under low light it stretches with longer internodes, while strong direct light yields compact, bushy growth and lets it serve as a trimmed foreground carpet. CO2 is sometimes called 'optional' by retailers (Buce, Modern Aquarium) but Aquasabi reports growth 'significantly decreases without supplementation' — pressurised CO2 is strongly recommended for a tight, dense form. A nutrient-rich substrate and regular column fertilisation with iron are advised. The plant is highly versatile across the layout: constant pruning keeps it low and spreads horizontal runners suitable for foreground/midground, while leaving it untrimmed allows vertical growth into background bushes. Initial adaptation can be slow but specimens become undemanding once established. Aquasabi notes that light deficiency causes lower-leaf drop and small leaf holes — these are diagnostic of insufficient PAR.
breedingNotes: Propagation is vegetative and simple: trim healthy stem tops just above a node, group several cuttings together, and replant them in the substrate ~1–2 cm apart, where they will root and branch within days. Mature plants can also be split, or daughter plants removed from established mats and replanted to thicken the bed.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Buce Plant product page for Micranthemum umbrosum (Giant Baby Tears). Provided: family Scrophulariaceae (older sense; now Linderniaceae), height 2–6 in (~5–15 cm), medium light, CO2 optional (~6–14 ppm beneficial), medium growth, North America origin, easy care, propagation via stem cuttings, versatile foreground/midground/background placement, and the comparison to Pearl Weed with slightly larger and rounder leaves.

additional.name: Modern Aquarium
additional.notes: Cross-checked: high light (especially for low lawn-like growth), CO2 not required but beneficial, medium-to-fast growth, temperature 72–86 °F (≈22–30 °C), foreground placement (also floating), easy-to-moderate care, propagation via cuttings replanted in plugs.

additional.name: Aquasabi Plantbase
additional.notes: Cross-checked: family Linderniaceae, common name 'Shade mudflower / Giant baby tears', medium difficulty, foreground/midground placement (also nano), fast growth, 10–30 cm care height (used as upper bound), medium-to-strong lighting (≥0.5 W/L), pH 5–7, gH 0–30 °dGH, kH 0–10 °dKH, CO2 recommended (growth significantly decreases without). Slow initial adaptation, then undemanding.

additional.name: Tropica
additional.notes: Cross-checked: 'medium' difficulty, elegant small-leaved stem plant, moderate growth, vivid bright green colour, suitable for small aquariums. Tropica did not surface a public detail page with parameter numbers, so we lean on Aquasabi and Modern Aquarium for the numeric values.

additional.name: Wikipedia (genus Micranthemum)
additional.notes: Used only for taxonomy fallback: confirmed genus Micranthemum in family Linderniaceae, order Lamiales, with native range 'Eastern USA to Tropical and Subtropical America'. Species page for M. umbrosum specifically was not available.
